rationale: The citing paper centers on flat-rate vs. pay-per-use tariff biases and explicitly tests behavioral mechanisms (e.g., taxi meter/usage-related effects) and downstream consumption/profit consequences. The cited paper provides a core mechanism—price bundling/transaction decoupling and weakened sunk-cost pressure affecting service consumption—so it is plausibly a substantive theoretical source rather than a generic mention, despite only DOI-level match evidence.
